prompt: Two instances that should be equal are treated as different by a HashMap. What is the bug?
choices: HashMap requires keys to implement Comparable
equals is overridden but hashCode is not; two equal Keys have different hash codes so HashMap puts them in different buckets
new Key(1) creates a new object which cannot be found in the map
HashMap.get uses == instead of equals to find keys
explanation: The contract: if a.equals(b) then a.hashCode() == b.hashCode(). Key overrides equals but inherits Object.hashCode() which uses object identity. Two separate new Key(1) objects have different hashCodes, so HashMap never finds the entry. Fix: @Override public int hashCode() { return id; }
